## reference · trustworthy design credibility signals ecommerce transaction trust reviews verification honest pricing
* A website's design quality is crucial for establishing trust, including a well-organized site structure and appropriate color scheme.
* Typos, broken links, and other mistakes can quickly degrade credibility.
* Upfront disclosure of information, such as contact details, pricing, and policies, is essential for building trust.
* Hiding information or requiring users to fill out lengthy forms to access it can lead to a loss of trust.
* Comprehensive, correct, and current content is necessary to demonstrate expertise and authority.
* Photos and images should be relevant and show the actual service or product, rather than just the end result.
* The website should represent the full range of services or products offered, to avoid alienating users looking for specific items.
* A clean and well-organized design, with adequate white space, can add to the perception of quality and trustworthiness.
* Colors and imagery should be used strategically to support the tone and brand of the organization.
* Login walls and gated content can degrade trust and turn users away.
* Asking for personal information before allowing users to explore the site can create a negative impression.
* Sites should provide easy access to important information, such as FAQs and contact details, to add to a feeling of transparency.
Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/trustworthy-design/
